concerns.text: T019 mandates generating 'synthetic dialogue turns' for classifier training. This violates FR-002 and the Constitution's 'Data Hygiene' principle. FR-002 requires the classifier to be trained on 'metadata tags... distinct from... the consensus-gap evaluation metric'. Generating synthetic text to train a classifier that then labels the *same* synthetic trajectories creates a circular dependency (synthetic text -> synthetic label -> synthetic injection), violating the requirement for independent ground truth and introducing a silent relaxation of the 'real-call testing' intent of FR-007.

T024 and T025 describe running the classifier 'every N turns' on streaming dialogue. However, T012/T014 only define the generation of complete `ConflictTrajectory` objects (static history). There is no task to implement the 'turn-by-turn' slicing logic or the streaming state management required to feed the classifier incrementally during the experiment. This is a semantic weakening of FR-002's 'every few turns' requirement, reducing it to a batch operation on static data.

T039 (Sensitivity Analysis) depends on `experiment_logs.json` (T028) containing 'confidence score' and 'injected state' per turn. T028 is currently unchecked/missing. Without a verified task to capture per-turn confidence scores in the logs, T039 cannot fulfill SC-005's requirement to 'sweep classifier confidence thresholds'. This is a dropped acceptance criterion for SC-005.

T041 requests 'Code cleanup... to ensure CPU memory usage stays <7GB'. This is a non-verifiable task with no concrete artifact or test condition. It silently relaxes the hard constraint of SC-003 (which requires explicit measurement of throughput/latency) into a vague 'cleanup' activity. A task claiming to satisfy a Success Criterion must have a verifiable deliverable (e.g., a memory profile report or a passing unit test), not just 'cleanup'.

T003 is marked `[X]` (complete) but the 'Re-plan' section in the artifact text explicitly states T003 was 'rejected due to missing configuration files'. This internal contradiction violates the 'Single Source of Truth' principle (Constitution Principle IV) and creates a false sense of compliance for a foundational setup task.
